reason: The definition of Spec(A) uses dim(F_{S(A)}(phi)) without specifying what 'dimension' means for faces. For finite-dimensional faces in a convex set, the dimension is standard (affine dimension). But for infinite-dimensional faces, the node includes {infinity} in the codomain. It would be helpful to specify: does dim mean affine dimension (the smallest dimension of an affine subspace containing the face)? Or topological dimension? For the commutant reformulation dim_R(pi_phi(A)'^{sa}) - 1, this is the real-linear dimension of a vector space, which is well-defined. Confirming that these two notions of dimension agree (which they do via the Alfsen-Shultz isomorphism when the face is finite-dimensional, and both give infinity otherwise) would strengthen the definition.
